ops.title: The four webnav-spike web gaps shipped — design decisions (fixed-stride paging, liteparse PDF lane, printed-number resolver) and the traps three review rounds surfaced
ops.body:
Shipped to main 2026-07-10 (commits `776ed63` content-type + PDF, `a62c08a` resolution + paging, `2fed54a` skill docs), fixing all four [[webnav-pageindex-spike]] gaps in [[ccx-web-op-family]]. Design decisions a later session should not re-litigate:

**Fixed-stride paging won after line-snapping failed two adversarial rounds.** `web read --offset` (token units, charsPerToken=4) serves exactly `span[offset*4:(offset+budget)*4]`, next = offset+budget, both bounds snapped BACKWARD to rune starts (walk-back bounded to `utf8.UTFMax-1` — unbounded walk let a run of 0x80 continuation bytes defeat the budget). Line-snap + kept-derived-next math is provably lossy: token-granular offsets cannot both advance and avoid skipping when a page serves <4 content bytes; forward-snap eats whole lines; overhead bytes (note/nav) skew blind math. Pages butt-join byte-exactly and may cut MID-LINE — deliberate. Tests assert reconstruction (`join(pages) == span`), not marker presence (stride splits markers).

**Budget+offset for OpWebRead lives in web.Run; render.Finalize passes it through.** The old "web.Run returns uncapped, Finalize caps" invariant can't do correct continuation math (Finalize sees rendered output incl. note/header/nav, not span bytes). Budget 0 = uncapped (codeexec path). Render order: resolve-note → content → footer → nav, all but content outside the budget.

**PDF lane**: plainHTTP `detectBodyKind` (mime.ParseMediaType, http.DetectContentType fallback; challengeSignature check stays FIRST) → `parsePDF` uv subprocess `--with liteparse==2.5.0` cloning embed.go's driver pattern. liteparse's `.parse()` returns `ParseResult` (markdown in `.text`, NOT a string) and chatters `[liteparse]` to stdout — driver must protect fd 1 (embed_driver.py pattern). The 20s plainHTTP deadline scopes the HTTP request only; parse runs under the cascade ctx (~120s effective, pdf.go's 5-min timeout is aspirational — raising it needs cascade-deadline surgery). Setpgid + pgid-SIGKILL in cmd.Cancel (ESRCH → os.ErrProcessDone) in pdf.go AND embed.go (shared orphan flaw).

**Printed-number resolver** matches `printedNumberRe` against `plainTitle(Section.Title)` — titles carry raw markdown (RFC 9110: `[5.6.7.](#section-5.6.7) [Date/Time Formats]`), so link-unwrap (incl. one paren-nesting level + backslash escapes + reference-style) and `*`/`` ` ``/`_` stripping happen before matching AND in the note display. Deliberate: undefined-ref forms resolve (visible-text contract); `_`-strip display cosmetics accepted; heading-TEXT matching excluded (hybrid search owns it).

Process lesson: **bare /tmp temp files in codex-wrapper runs collide across concurrent sessions** — a refuter's reply file got clobbered by another session's cc-notes review mid-run. Codex prompts now pin temp files to the session scratchpad + an on-topic sanity check on the reply. Also: two parallel implementation agents editing the same Go package must get isolated worktrees (`isolation: 'worktree'`, commit, cherry-pick back) — a shared tree poisons each other's `go test` with mid-edit compile breakage.
